title: Commission de toponymie du Quebec

infobox.value: This article about an organization in Canada is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Commission_de_toponymie_du_Qu%C3%A9bec&action=edit).

(French pronunciation: [[kɔmisjɔ̃ də tɔpɔnimi dy kebɛk]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), *Toponymy Commission of Québec*) is the [Government of Québec](/wiki/Government_of_Qu%C3%A9bec "Government of Québec")'s public body responsible for cataloging, preserving, making official and publicizing Québec's place names and their origins according to the province's toponymy rules.

Commission de toponymie of Québec. ["Commission de toponymie"](https://web.archive.org/web/20101123002654/http://www.toponymie.gouv.qc.ca/ct/english.html). Government of Québec. Archived from [the original](http://www.toponymie.gouv.qc.ca/ct/english.html) on 23 November 2010. Retrieved 23 December 2010.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Commission de toponymie du Quebec](/w/index.php?title=Commission_de_toponymie_du_Quebec&redirect=no "Commission de toponymie du Quebec"))

Public body responsible for the management of Quebec place names

The **Commission de toponymie du Québec** (French pronunciation: [[kɔmisjɔ̃ də tɔpɔnimi dy kebɛk]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), *Toponymy Commission of Québec*) is the [Government of Québec](/wiki/Government_of_Qu%C3%A9bec "Government of Québec")'s public body responsible for cataloging, preserving, making official and publicizing Québec's place names and their origins according to the province's toponymy rules.
It also provides recommendations to the government with regard to toponymic changes.

Its mandate covers the namings of:

* natural geographical features (lakes, rivers, mountains, etc.)
* constructed features (dams, embankments, bridges, etc.)
* administrative units (wildlife sanctuaries, administrative regions, parks, etc.)
* inhabited areas (villages, towns, [Indian](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_in_Canada "Indigenous peoples in Canada") reserves, etc.)
* roadways (streets, roads, boulevards, etc.)

A child agency of the [Office québécois de la langue française](/wiki/Office_qu%C3%A9b%C3%A9cois_de_la_langue_fran%C3%A7aise "Office québécois de la langue française"), it was created in 1977 through jurisdiction defined in the [Charter of the French Language](/wiki/Charter_of_the_French_Language "Charter of the French Language") to replace the Commission of Geography, created in 1912.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
